title: Meridian Hedge Fund Access Program
content:
MERIDIAN HEDGE FUND ACCESS PROGRAM - CONFIDENTIAL CLIENT FACTSHEET

The Meridian Hedge Fund Access Program provides qualified private wealth clients with curated access to institutional-quality hedge fund strategies typically reserved for ultra-high-net-worth investors and institutional allocators. This program is exclusively available to clients meeting Tier 4 private wealth status with minimum investable assets of $10 million.

INVESTMENT OVERVIEW
Our platform currently features 12 carefully vetted hedge fund managers across multiple strategies including long/short equity, event-driven, relative value, and global macro. Each fund has been selected based on rigorous due diligence encompassing risk management protocols, operational infrastructure, and performance attribution analysis spanning minimum 5-year track records. The program offers both direct fund access and our proprietary Meridian Multi-Manager Portfolio (MMP), which provides diversified exposure across 6-8 strategies with initial minimums of $2 million versus typical $5-25 million direct fund minimums.

PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS
Historical performance data through December 2024 shows the MMP has delivered annualized net returns of 8.7% over the trailing 5-year period, with maximum drawdown of 4.2% and Sharpe ratio of 1.34. These metrics compare favorably to the HFRI Fund Weighted Composite Index while maintaining lower volatility than traditional equity allocations. Individual fund performance ranges from 6.2% to 14.8% annualized returns, with varying correlation patterns to public markets.

RISK CONSIDERATIONS
This program carries significant risks appropriate only for sophisticated investors. Hedge funds employ leverage, derivatives, and complex trading strategies that may result in substantial losses. Liquidity constraints include quarterly redemption periods with 60-90 day notice requirements, and some strategies impose initial lock-up periods of 12-24 months. Market disruption events may trigger gate provisions limiting redemption capacity to 10-25% of fund assets quarterly.

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE
All funds operate under SEC Regulation D private placement exemptions, limiting participation to accredited investors as defined under Rule 501. Our investment committee conducts suitability analysis pursuant to FINRA Rule 2111, ensuring recommendations align with client investment objectives, risk tolerance, and overall portfolio construction. Due to the private nature of these investments, clients receive quarterly reporting rather than daily pricing, and positions are marked to estimated fair value.

FEE STRUCTURE
Hedge fund fees typically follow a "2 and 20" structure with 2% annual management fees plus 20% performance fees above high-water marks. Meridian charges an additional 0.50% annual overlay fee for due diligence, monitoring, and client reporting services. The MMP structure negotiates reduced fees averaging 1.75% management and 17.5% performance fees, plus our 0.50% overlay, providing meaningful cost savings versus direct fund access.

SUITABILITY ASSESSMENT
This program is designed for clients seeking portfolio diversification beyond traditional asset classes, with capacity to commit capital for extended periods and tolerance for reduced liquidity. Typical allocation recommendations range from 10-25% of total investment assets, integrated within broader strategic asset allocation frameworks. Clients must acknowledge understanding of hedge fund risks and maintain adequate liquid reserves for near-term cash flow needs.

For additional information and current fund availability, please contact your Private Wealth Advisor. All investments require completion of subscription documents and suitability verification prior to funding.
